abstract:
This article presents findings from a Research through Design investigation focusing on a reflexive approach to data curation and the use of generative AI in design and creative practices. Using binary gender categories manifested in children’s toys as a context, we examine three design experiments aimed at probing how designers can cultivate a
            reflexive human-AI practice
            to confront and challenge their internalized biases. Our goal is to underscore the intricate interplay between the designer, AI technology, and publicly held imaginaries and to offer an initial set of tactics for how personal biases and societal norms can be illuminated through interactions with AI. We conclude by proposing that designers not only bear the responsibility of grappling critically with the complexities of AI but also possess the opportunity to creatively harness the limitations of technology to craft a
            reflexive data curation
            that encourages profound reflections and awareness within design processes.

affiliation: Department of Software Technology, TU Delft, Delft, The Netherlands
authors_full.name: Anne Arzberger

affiliation: Department of Architecture and Design, Politecnico di Torino, Turin, Italy
authors_full.name: Maria Luce Lupetti

affiliation: Department of Design, Politecnico di Milano, Milan, Italy
authors_full.name: Elisa Giaccardi

ur.ai_reason: 研究生成式AI与设计师的反思性协作；含三项设计实验，HCI研究方法
ur.tldr: 采用研究性设计方法，以儿童玩具的二元性别分类为情境，开展三项设计实验，探索生成式AI在数据整理中的反思性实践，揭示AI如何帮助设计师察觉并挑战自身性别偏见，提出反思性数据整理策略。

title: Reflexive Data Curation: Opportunities and Challenges for Embracing Uncertainty in Human–AI Collaboration
title_zh: 反思性数据管理：人机协作中拥抱不确定性的机遇与挑战
